title: If Beale Street Could Talk
logline: A young pregnant woman fights to prove her boyfriend's innocence while he sits in jail awaiting trial for a rape he didn't commit, as their families struggle to keep their love and hope alive against a racist justice system.
antagonistOrConflict: Systemic racism and the criminal justice system

actBreaks.description: The story shifts from establishing the romance and pregnancy revelation to focusing on the legal battle and investigation.

validation.ruling: Scene #24 shows Hayward revealing that Mrs. Rogers has disappeared and they need expensive special investigators to find her, fundamentally shifting the story from legal defense to a desperate search mission and raising the stakes significantly.

actBreaks.description: Sharon departs for Puerto Rico to find Victoria, marking the climactic attempt to secure Fonny's freedom.

validation.ruling: Scene #55 shows Tish going into labor during the prison visit with Fonny, creating an irreversible crisis where their child will be born while he remains imprisoned, forcing the story into its final desperate phase.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This screenplay employs a sophisticated non-linear structure that weaves between present-day prison visits and extended flashbacks revealing how Tish and Fonny's relationship developed. The narrative moves fluidly through time, using the pregnancy announcement as an anchor point that grounds us in the present while exploring the past. The structure mirrors the emotional journey - we experience the love story in reverse, understanding the depth of what's at stake as we see how it was built. The three-act progression moves from revelation and family dynamics, through investigation and mounting pressure, to the desperate final attempt at justice.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately contemplative, with longer scenes allowing relationships to breathe and develop. Montage sequences (scenes 4-8, 11-14, 40-45) compress time effectively while maintaining emotional continuity. The Puerto Rico sequence (scenes 56-69) creates sustained tension through Sharon's mission, while the final act slows to focus on intimate moments between Tish and Fonny as hope dwindles.

incitingIncident.description: At a vegetable stand, an Italian punk sexually harasses Tish, grabbing and touching her, until she slaps him and Fonny arrives to beat him up and drag him outside.

validation.ruling: Scene #1 is exposition establishing the current situation rather than the event that shattered their ordinary world. Scene #49 shows the Italian punk sexually harassing Tish at the vegetable stand, leading to Fonny beating him up - this violent confrontation directly triggers the false rape accusation that destroys their lives and sends Fonny to prison, making it the true inciting incident.

midpoint.description: Fonny and Tish tour a Canal Street loft with landlord Levy, finding it big, in good condition, and surprisingly affordable with only one month's deposit required.

validation.ruling: Scene #46 is a quiet moment about finding an apartment, lacking the dramatic intensity for a midpoint. Scene #49 shows the violent confrontation where Fonny beats up the Italian punk who harassed Tish - this is the point of no return that triggers the false rape accusation, fundamentally shifting the story from their hopeful future to fighting for Fonny's freedom.

climax.description: Sharon finds Victoria, a pregnant blonde girl, in a makeshift apartment and asks for Mrs. Rogers, but Victoria denies knowing anyone by that name and asks Sharon to leave.

validation.ruling: Scene #69 shows Sharon's confrontation with Victoria in the Puerto Rican barrio, where she presents the photograph and Victoria denies being Mrs. Rogers - this is the peak dramatic moment where their last hope for Fonny's freedom hangs in the balance, representing the ultimate confrontation to resolve the main story question.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YORK CITY CORRECTIONAL FACILITY - DAY
scenes.summary: Tish visits Fonny in jail and tells him through the glass partition that they are going to have a baby, while he processes this life-changing news in stunned silence.
scenes.turningPointNote: The pregnancy announcement establishes the central stakes and urgency driving the entire narrative

scenes.heading: INT. TISH’S HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Tish sits alone in her family's Harlem apartment drinking tea when her mother Sharon arrives home with shopping bags.

scenes.heading: INT. TISH’S HOME, TISH’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In Tish's bedroom, Sharon comforts her crying daughter about the pregnancy, reassuring her that she's not a bad girl and that she and Fonny would be together if not for his imprisonment.

scenes.heading: INT. HARLEM VOCATIONAL SCHOOL - DAY - MONTAGE 1
scenes.summary: A younger Fonny works at a table-saw in vocational school, making furniture that nobody will buy.

scenes.heading: INT. HARLEM VOCATIONAL SCHOOL - NIGHT - MONTAGE 1
scenes.summary: Fonny steals wood from the vocational school workshop at night, loading it into a pickup truck.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT KITCHEN - NIGHT - MONTAGE 1
scenes.summary: Fonny works as a short-order cook in a barbecue restaurant kitchen, wiping sweat from his face.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT APARTMENT - DAY - MONTAGE 1
scenes.summary: In his basement apartment, Fonny works on wood sculptures including a tormented figure of a naked man that he gives to Tish's mother.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT APARTMENT - DAY - MONTAGE 1
scenes.summary: Fonny continues woodworking in his basement while still images show the drug-fueled urban blight affecting young Black Americans who have been told they are worthless.

scenes.heading: INT. TISH’S HOME, LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tish's sister Ernestine arrives home and discusses Tish's condition and upcoming doctor's appointment with their parents Sharon and Joseph.

scenes.heading: INT. TISH’S HOME - NIGHT - LATER
scenes.summary: The family gathers around the dinner table as Sharon brings out special French brandy and Joseph makes a toast to celebrate Tish's pregnancy.

scenes.heading: EXT. TISH’S HOME - DAY - MONTAGE 2
scenes.summary: Sharon stands at the top of her stoop while Fonny waits at the bottom and Tish stands between them on the day he gave Sharon the sculpture.

scenes.heading: EXT. HARLEM, SIDEWALK - DAY - MONTAGE 2
scenes.summary: Tish and Fonny walk hand in hand down a Harlem sidewalk, with Fonny dressed in a lumberjack coat and heavy shoes.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBWAY CAR - DAY - MONTAGE 2
scenes.summary: On a crowded subway train, Tish and Fonny stand wedged together in a corner, with Tish staring at Fonny's lips as he speaks.

scenes.heading: EXT. WASHINGTON SQUARE PARK - DAY - MONTAGE 2
scenes.summary: In Washington Square Park at dusk, Fonny tells Tish he sometimes slept there and offers to show her his new apartment downtown.

scenes.heading: INT. TISH’S HOME, FRONT DOOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Hunt family arrives at Tish's home for a meeting, with Mrs. Hunt and her daughters overdressed and creating an awkward atmosphere as they gather in the living room.

scenes.heading: INT. SPANISH RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At a Spanish restaurant, Fonny speaks Spanish with the server Pedrocito while Tish watches in surprise, having never heard him speak the language before.

scenes.heading: EXT. AVE A AND ST. MARKS - NIGHT - MOVING
scenes.summary: Walking on the street at night, Fonny asks Tish to come see his place and she agrees despite the late hour.

scenes.heading: INT. TISH’S HOME, LIVING ROOM - NIGHT - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Tish announces to both families that she and Fonny are going to have a baby, causing varied reactions including Mrs. Hunt looking away and Frank and Joe turning to each other.
scenes.turningPointNote: The formal announcement to both families creates the central family conflict and sets up the opposing reactions

scenes.heading: INT. BANK STREET FLAT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In Fonny's basement apartment, he drapes a Mexican shawl over Tish's head and shoulders, calling her a rose in Spanish Harlem, and promises to get her a real rose next week.

scenes.heading: INT. BANK STREET FLAT - NIGHT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: After making love for the first time, Fonny and Tish lie naked together discussing the physical aftermath and her virginity.
scenes.turningPointNote: Their first sexual encounter deepens their relationship and sets the stage for the pregnancy

scenes.heading: INT. SUBWAY CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Fonny and Tish stand together on a mostly empty subway car, with Fonny protectively holding her.

scenes.heading: EXT. TISH’S HOME - DAY (DAWN)
scenes.summary: Fonny and Tish arrive at her stoop at dawn holding hands, where Ernestine greets them at the door with a knowing smile.

scenes.heading: INT. TISH’S HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Fonny blurts out that he and Tish want to get married as they enter her home, surprising both Ernestine and Tish, while Sharon prepares coffee for everyone.
scenes.turningPointNote: Fonny's spontaneous marriage proposal escalates their commitment and family involvement

scenes.heading: INT. LAW OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: In lawyer Hayward's office, Tish and Sharon learn that the key witness Mrs. Rogers has disappeared, making Fonny's case much more difficult.
scenes.turningPointNote: The disappearance of the crucial witness dramatically worsens Fonny's legal situation

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YORK CITY CORRECTIONAL FACILITY - DAY
scenes.summary: During a jail visit, Fonny angrily questions how they will find Mrs. Rogers and accuses the DA's office of helping her disappear, while expressing desperation about his situation in prison.

scenes.heading: EXT. SEVENTH AVENUE - DAY
scenes.summary: Fonny and his friend Daniel laugh and greet each other enthusiastically on Seventh Avenue after running into each other.

scenes.heading: INT. BANK STREET FLAT - DAY
scenes.summary: Daniel and Fonny enter Fonny's apartment still laughing, with Tish looking on in surprise as they exchange greetings.

scenes.heading: EXT. BANK STREET - DAY - MOVING
scenes.summary: Tish walks along Bank Street carrying a shopping bag.

scenes.heading: INT. BANK STREET FLAT - DAY
scenes.summary: Daniel and Fonny drink beer and smoke while discussing Fonny's marriage plans and the difficulty of finding housing due to racial discrimination.

scenes.heading: INT. BANK STREET FLAT - DAY - LATER
scenes.summary: Daniel tells Fonny about his recent release from prison after serving two years for something he didn't do, explaining the brutal reality of the justice system.

scenes.heading: INT. BANK STREET FLAT - NIGHT - LATER
scenes.summary: The three friends share a feast that Tish prepared, with Daniel complimenting her cooking and joking about visiting more often.

scenes.heading: INT. TISH’S HOME, BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sharon sits beside a sleeping Tish who is having a nightmare, gently waking her and comforting her with a damp towel while speaking about suffering.

scenes.heading: INT. TISH’S HOME, KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: In the kitchen, Ernestine calls Tish "Jezebel" while looking through paperwork as Tish gets coffee and hugs Sharon.

scenes.heading: INT. 1 TRAIN, DOWNTOWN - DAY - MOVING
scenes.summary: Tish rides the subway alone to work, visibly pregnant and isolated in the crowded car.

scenes.heading: INT. LORD AND TAYLORS - DAY - MOVING
scenes.summary: Tish hurries through Lord and Taylor's main floor past her white coworkers to clock in for work.

scenes.heading: INT. LORD AND TAYLORS - DAY - LATER
scenes.summary: Tish works behind the perfume counter with a forced smile, interacting with white customers while struggling to maintain her facade.

scenes.heading: INT. COLUMBUS CIRCLE PUB - DAY
scenes.summary: At a dive bar, Ernestine tells Tish they found Mrs. Rogers in Puerto Rico and someone needs to go there to talk to her, which they'll have to pay for.

scenes.heading: EXT. COLUMBUS CIRCLE PUB - DAY
scenes.summary: Tish and Ernestine leave the bar together with Ernestine's arm around her sister as they walk down the sidewalk.

scenes.heading: INT. 8TH AVE BERNIE’S - DAY - MOVING - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: In another bar, Joe and Frank discuss their financial struggles and Joe tells Frank to stop blaming himself and focus on getting Fonny out of prison.

scenes.heading: INT. HAYWARD’S OFFICE - DAY - MONTAGE 3
scenes.summary: Hayward stands behind his desk looking down at stacks of paperwork related to Fonny's case.

scenes.heading: INT. BAR AT THE RITZ CARLTON - DAY - MONTAGE 3
scenes.summary: Hayward drinks cognac at the Ritz Carlton bar surrounded by other lawyers, appearing out of place among the good old boys.

scenes.heading: INT. TISH’S HOME - NIGHT - MONTAGE 3
scenes.summary: The family engages in an inaudible spirited discussion at home while Tish narrates about their distrust of Hayward.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBWAY CAR - DAY - MOVING - MONTAGE 3
scenes.summary: A noticeably more pregnant Tish rides the subway to work wearing jackets and slacks that her mother has let out.

scenes.heading: INT. UPPER WEST SIDE APARTMENT - DAY - MONTAGE 3
scenes.summary: Ernestine works as a part-time secretary for a wealthy white actress while Joseph and Frank systematically steal from the docks and garment district to sell goods in Harlem.

scenes.heading: EXT. HARLEM SIDE STREET - DAY - MONTAGE 3
scenes.summary: Frank and Joe stand at the back of a van conducting business with other men, selling stolen goods to support the family financially.

scenes.heading: INT. CANAL STREET LOFT - DAY
scenes.summary: Fonny and Tish tour a Canal Street loft with landlord Levy, finding it big, in good condition, and surprisingly affordable with only one month's deposit required.
scenes.turningPointNote: Finding the perfect loft represents hope and the possibility of their future together

scenes.heading: EXT. CANAL STREET - DAY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Outside the loft, Levy explains the neighborhood to Fonny and Tish, and when Fonny asks about the catch, Levy reveals he doesn't care about their race.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLEECKER STREET - DAY - MOVING
scenes.summary: Walking through the Village at twilight, Fonny suddenly stops mid-stride and shouts joyfully to the heavens in celebration.

scenes.heading: EXT. VEGETABLE STAND - DAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: At a vegetable stand, an Italian punk sexually harasses Tish, grabbing and touching her, until she slaps him and Fonny arrives to beat him up and drag him outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLEECKER STREET - NIGHT - MOVING
scenes.summary: Walking home, Fonny tells Tish never to try to protect him again, explaining it's not the same as him protecting her, then smashes the bag of tomatoes against a wall.

scenes.heading: INT. SPANISH RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Fonny and Tish are seated at Pedrocito's restaurant where Fonny admits they have no money but promises payment in a couple days, leading to playful banter with Pedrocito who brings them margaritas.

scenes.heading: INT. BANK STREET FLAT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Fonny and Tish make love on their palette in the Bank Street flat, with Fonny whispering her name tenderly afterward.

scenes.heading: INT. TISH’S HOME, KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: A very pregnant Tish stands at the stove preparing a meal when the baby's kicks cause her to drop an egg, spill coffee, and react to perfume with nausea.

scenes.heading: INT. TISH’S HOME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tish appears from the bathroom after vomiting, clutching her stomach and forehead as she moves down the hallway to sit at the dining table.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YORK CITY CORRECTIONAL FACILITY - DAY
scenes.summary: Fonny jokes with a heavily pregnant Tish during their prison visit, calling her 'big as two houses' and suggesting they might have twins or triplets.

scenes.heading: EXT. SAN JUAN INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sharon deboards a Pan-Am jet at San Juan International Airport among dozens of other travelers.

scenes.heading: INT. SAN JUAN INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sharon stands out as the darkest, most American figure in the loud Spanish-speaking airport as she tries to communicate with an airport staffer who doesn't speak English.

scenes.heading: INT. JAIME’S CAB - NIGHT - MOVING
scenes.summary: Sharon rides in Jaime's cab through the alien Puerto Rican landscape, with both eyeing each other nervously in the rearview mirror until Jaime reassures her she's safe.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL DEL RICO, SUITE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sharon unpacks in her hotel suite, trying on a stylish hat in the mirror, putting it on and taking it off as she considers her reflection.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL DEL RICO, ELEVATOR - NIGHT - DESCENDING
scenes.summary: Sharon rides the hotel elevator down, staring into her own eyes in the mirrored door, notably not wearing the hat.

scenes.heading: INT. JAIME’S CAB - NIGHT - MOVING
scenes.summary: Sharon rides in Jaime's cab dressed ridiculously like an American tourist, and when their eyes meet in the rearview mirror this time, neither looks away as they share a chaste connection.

scenes.heading: INT. LA PIAZZA RESORT, LOBBY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sharon stands in the foyer of La Piazza Resort Hotel, positioned between the lobby behind her and the entrance to the subterranean nightclub before her.

scenes.heading: INT. DISCOTECA LA PIAZZA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sharon sits alone in the nightclub booth asking a server about meeting Pietro Alvarez on business, causing a reaction from the server who leaves to inform Alvarez while Sharon waits and drinks.

scenes.heading: EXT. RESORT LAPIAZZA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sharon marches past a waiting Jaime and gets into his cab, shocking him with her abrupt behavior.

scenes.heading: INT. JAIME’S CAB - NIGHT - SAME
scenes.summary: Sharon sobs in the backseat of Jaime's cab while he stands outside the window, allowing her this moment of breakdown.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL DEL RICO, SUITE - DAY
scenes.summary: Sharon gazes out her hotel window at the Puerto Rican sun and sees Jaime waiting in the parking lot below, having become her responsibility.

scenes.heading: INT. JAIME’S CAB - DAY - MOVING
scenes.summary: Sharon rides in Jaime's cab through a desolate San Juan slum as Jaime thinks he's found the location of the blonde woman they're seeking.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARRIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Jaime stands beside his cab watching Sharon approach the wretched squalor of the barrio, clearly not wanting to let her make this walk alone.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARRIO APARTMENT BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Sharon finds Victoria, a pregnant blonde girl, in a makeshift apartment and asks for Mrs. Rogers, but Victoria denies knowing anyone by that name and asks Sharon to leave.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sharon locates Victoria but the key witness denies her identity and refuses to cooperate, potentially destroying Fonny's defense

scenes.heading: INT. BANK STREET FLAT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Fonny works on a piece of brown wood in the Bank Street flat, terrified to touch it and waiting for the wood to speak before he can move, picking up and putting down his chisel.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YORK CITY CORRECTIONAL FACILITY, CELL - DAY
scenes.summary: Fonny lies in his prison cell with eyes wide, staring unblinking at the ceiling as he looks far off to some distant vision.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YORK CITY CORRECTIONAL FACILITY - DAY
scenes.summary: Tish tells a visibly gaunt and thinned Fonny during their prison visit that Sharon found Victoria but the girl went crazy and disappeared, so the trial might be postponed.
scenes.turningPointNote: The devastating news that their key witness has vanished destroys their hope for Fonny's defense

scenes.heading: EXT. 14TH ST. AND 8TH AVE - DAY
scenes.summary: A very pregnant Tish emerges from the subway carrying a paper sack, hurries to a crosswalk, and spots Officer Bell approaching up the avenue.

scenes.heading: INT. SPANISH RESTAURANT - DAY
scenes.summary: Tish sits alone at a back table in Pedrocito's restaurant staring at untouched paella until Pedrocito takes the plate and returns with a fresh hot one.

scenes.heading: INT. SEDAN - DAY - MOVING
scenes.summary: Pedrocito drives Tish home in his sedan, joking that her baby will be a boy, when Tish startles and looks with recognition at someone on the curb during a red light.

scenes.heading: INT. BANK STREET FLAT - DAY
scenes.summary: Tish sits in the Bank Street flat placing a block of wood on Fonny's work table when Adrienne calls crying, asking frantically about her father's whereabouts.
scenes.turningPointNote: Adrienne's panicked call about her missing father suggests another crisis is unfolding

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
